title: Reinforcement Learning From Imperfect Corrective Actions And Proxy Rewards
authors: Not specified in the provided text
abstract: This paper addresses the challenge of defining high-quality reward signals in reinforcement learning. When rewards are misspecified, RL agents learn unexpected and unwanted behaviors. The paper proposes a framework where RL agents learn from two sources: proxy rewards and corrective actions. Proxy rewards are imperfect reward functions that approximately specify the task, while corrective actions are provided by human labelers who can correct an agent's action by demonstrating a better one. The paper argues that learning from both sources simultaneously, even though both may be imperfect, can be beneficial.

methodology.approach: Learning from two sources of signals: proxy rewards and corrective actions
key_claims: Better align with human preferences than baseline methods
Can overcome non-optimality of corrective actions thanks to guidance from proxy rewards

results: The paper claims experimental validation showing benefits of learning from both proxy rewards and corrective actions simultaneously.
limitations_of_related_approaches: May require a reliable expert to provide correct labels
May assume the agent only learns from human feedback, which would require too many queries
